title: Risky Sign-In From Unknown Device and Rarely Used IP
description: Detects risky sign-in attempts from unmanaged/non-onboarded devices or IP addresses rarely used by the user. Classifies risk as High (new IP) or Medium (IP seen fewer than 3 distinct days in 29 days).
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Risky Sign-In From Unknown Device and Rarely Used IP
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// MITRE: T1078.004
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
let CorporateIPRange = "0.0.0.0/16";
let LookbackHistory = 29d;
let TodayStart = startofday(now());
let AffectedUsers = materialize(
    BehaviorAnalytics
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
    | where not(ipv4_is_in_range(SourceIPAddress, CorporateIPRange))
    | where isnotempty(UserPrincipalName)
    | where ActivityType != "FailedLogOn"
    | where ActivityInsights.FirstTimeUserConnectedFromDevice == true
    | join kind=inner (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountUpn | project AccountUpn, TenantMembershipType) on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.AccountUpn
    | where TenantMembershipType == "Member"
    | distinct UserPrincipalName);
let HistoricalIPs = EntraIdSignInEvents
    | where TimeGenerated between ((TodayStart - LookbackHistory) .. (TodayStart - 1ms))
    | where AccountUpn in (AffectedUsers) | where ErrorCode == 0
    | summarize DistinctDaysSeenFromIP = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)) by AccountUpn, IPAddress
    | project UserPrincipalName = AccountUpn, SourceIPAddress = IPAddress, DistinctDaysSeenFromIP;
BehaviorAnalytics
| where TimeGenerated > ago(4h)
| where not(ipv4_is_in_range(SourceIPAddress, CorporateIPRange))
| where isnotempty(UserPrincipalName) | where ActivityType != "FailedLogOn"
| where ActivityInsights.FirstTimeUserConnectedFromDevice == true
| join kind=inner hint.strategy=broadcast (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountUpn | project AccountUpn, TenantMembershipType) on $left.UserPrincipalName == $right.AccountUpn
| where TenantMembershipType == "Member"
| extend SourceDeviceLower = tolower(SourceDevice)
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceInfo | extend DeviceNameLower = tolower(DeviceName) | project DeviceNameLower, DeviceName, DeviceId, OnboardingStatus) on $left.SourceDeviceLower == $right.DeviceNameLower
| where OnboardingStatus != "Onboarded" or isempty(OnboardingStatus)
| join kind=leftouter hint.strategy=broadcast (HistoricalIPs) on UserPrincipalName, SourceIPAddress
| extend IPRiskClassification = case(isempty(DistinctDaysSeenFromIP), "High Risk - New IP", DistinctDaysSeenFromIP < 3, "Medium Risk - Infrequent IP", "Low Risk")
| where IPRiskClassification != "Low Risk"
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by UserPrincipalName
| where isempty(DeviceName) | where DistinctDaysSeenFromIP < 2

title: Detection Rule Usefulness Evaluation (DOVE Model)
description: Evaluates detection rule overlap: finds alerts firing on the same assets within the same hour from multiple sources including custom detections.
author: Sergio Albea

title: APT28 Kill Chain: LNK to UNC to SMB Credential Exfiltration
description: Detects APT28 multi-stage chain (CVE-2026-32202): LNK initial access → CPL/DLL/EXE via UNC path → NTLM exfiltration over SMB to non-private IP.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Copilot Jailbreak Detected
description: Retrieves Copilot interaction events where a jailbreak attempt was detected. Maps to MITRE ATLAS AML.T0054.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Git Abuse High Fidelity — Repository Tampering
description: High-confidence detection correlating Git identity changes, commit amendments, force pushes, and time manipulation on the same device within 30 minutes.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Privileged RDP Session Source Mismatch
description: Detects RDP logons with privileged accounts from devices where the account owner has not recently logged in, indicating lateral movement or credential theft.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Audit B2B Guest Devices Trust Type
description: Audits B2B guest sign-ins grouped by device trust type to identify unmanaged or unknown devices used by external guest accounts.
author: Jay Kerai
content:
// Title: Audit B2B Guest Devices Trust Type
// Author: Jay Kerai
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where UserType == "Guest"
| extend TrustType = tostring(DeviceDetail.trustType)
| extend DeviceOS = tostring(DeviceDetail.operatingSystem)
| extend IsManaged = tostring(DeviceDetail.isManaged)
| summarize SignInCount = count(), UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress)
    by UserPrincipalName, TrustType, DeviceOS, IsManaged, ResultType
| extend DeviceRisk = case(
    TrustType == "", "Unregistered Device",
    TrustType == "AzureAD", "Cloud Joined",
    TrustType == "Hybrid", "Hybrid Joined",
    "Other")
| order by SignInCount desc

title: SharePoint Phishing Click + Verification Code + Suspicious Login
description: Multi-stage detection correlating a SharePoint phishing URL click, a one-time verification code email, and a subsequent suspicious Entra ID sign-in.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Post-Phishing URL Click Suspicious Sign-Ins
description: Correlates Entra ID sign-in events that follow a phishing URL click in email, flagging suspicious logins occurring within 60 minutes of the click.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: High-Privilege Takeover: Agent ID Administrator Role Abuse
description: Detects assignment of the Agent ID Administrator role in Entra ID audit logs, which can be abused for high-privilege takeover scenarios.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Session IDs from Multiple OS and User Agents (Token Theft Detection)
description: Detects the same Entra ID session being used from multiple operating systems or user agents, a strong indicator of token theft and session hijacking.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Session IDs from Multiple OS and User Agents (Token Theft)
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// MITRE: T1539 - Steal Web Session Cookie
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
EntraIdSignInEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where isnotempty(SessionId) and isnotempty(AccountUpn)
| summarize
    UniqueOSCount = dcount(OSPlatform),
    UniqueUACount = dcount(UserAgent),
    OSList = make_set(OSPlatform),
    UAList = make_set(UserAgent),
    IPList = make_set(IPAddress),
    SignInCount = count()
    by SessionId, AccountUpn
| where UniqueOSCount > 1 or UniqueUACount > 2
| extend Severity = case(UniqueOSCount > 1, "High", UniqueUACount > 2, "Medium", "Low")
| order by UniqueOSCount desc, UniqueUACount desc

title: Multiple Unusual Network Adapter Vendors on Device
description: Identifies devices with multiple unusual or potentially suspicious network adapter vendors, which may indicate rogue hardware or network interception.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Multiple Unusual Network Adapter Vendors
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
let KnownVendors = dynamic(["Intel", "Realtek", "Broadcom", "Qualcomm", "Microsoft", "VMware", "Hyper-V"]);
DeviceNetworkInfo
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| mv-expand NetworkAdapters = parse_json(NetworkAdapters)
| extend Vendor = tostring(NetworkAdapters.Vendor)
| where isnotempty(Vendor)
| extend IsKnownVendor = iff(Vendor has_any (KnownVendors), true, false)
| summarize
    UnknownVendors = make_set_if(Vendor, IsKnownVendor == false),
    UnknownVendorCount = dcountif(Vendor, IsKnownVendor == false),
    AllVendors = make_set(Vendor)
    by DeviceId, DeviceName
| join kind=inner (DeviceInfo | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | summarize arg_max(Timestamp,*) by DeviceId | project DeviceId, OSPlatform, PublicIP) on DeviceId
| where UnknownVendorCount >= 2
| order by UnknownVendorCount desc

title: New Tenant Allow/Block List (TABL) Entry
description: Detects new entries added to the Tenant Allow/Block List in Microsoft 365 Defender, which could be used to whitelist malicious senders or URLs.
author: Jay Kerai

title: Intune Operational Logs: Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
description: Detects unexpected changes to device OS type in Intune operational logs, which may indicate device spoofing or policy tampering.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Intune Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
IntuneOperationalLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Update", "Modify")
| extend Properties = parse_json(Properties)
| extend DeviceId = tostring(Properties.IntuneDeviceId)
| extend OldOS = tostring(Properties.OldValue)
| extend NewOS = tostring(Properties.NewValue)
| where isnotempty(OldOS) and isnotempty(NewOS)
| where OldOS != NewOS
| where NewOS has_any ("Windows", "iOS", "Android", "macOS")
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceId, OldOS, NewOS, OperationName, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: Multiple Registered Devices By Account
description: Identifies accounts that have registered an unusual number of devices across Entra ID, Intune, and identity logs — a potential indicator of device registration abuse.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Multiple Registered Devices By Account
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Register device", "Add device")
| extend Initiator = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| extend DeviceId = tostring(TargetResources[0].id)
| where isnotempty(Initiator)
| summarize DeviceCount = dcount(DeviceId), DeviceIds = make_set(DeviceId) by Initiator, bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| where DeviceCount >= 3
| join kind=leftouter (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountUpn | project AccountUpn, Department, JobTitle) on $left.Initiator == $right.AccountUpn
| order by DeviceCount desc

title: Detect Potential Malicious Emails via InternetMessageId Dates
description: Identifies potentially malicious emails by detecting anomalies in InternetMessageId date components, which can reveal spoofed or manipulated email headers.
author: Sergio Albea

title: Detect Microsoft OTP Emails via InternetMessageId ODSPNOTIFY
description: Identifies Microsoft one-time passcode emails sent as part of SharePoint/OneDrive sharing flows, useful for tracking external sharing activity.
author: Sergio Albea
content:
// Title: Detect Microsoft OTP Emails via InternetMessageId ODSPNOTIFY
// Author: Sergio Albea
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
EmailEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InternetMessageId has "odspnotify"
| where Subject has_any ("verification code", "one-time", "passcode", "OTP")
| summarize
    EmailCount = count(),
    Recipients = make_set(RecipientEmailAddress, 10),
    Senders = make_set(SenderFromAddress, 5)
    by bin(Timestamp, 1d)
| order by EmailCount desc

title: Detect Microsoft Shared File Messages via InternetMessageId ODSPNOTIFY
description: Detects Microsoft shared file notification emails by their InternetMessageId pattern, useful for auditing external file sharing.
author: Sergio Albea

title: Audit Logs: Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
description: Detects OS type changes for devices in Entra ID audit logs, which may indicate device record tampering or spoofing.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos
content:
// Title: Audit Logs: Unexpected Device OS Type Modification
// Author: Jose Sebastian Canos
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where OperationName has_any ("Update device", "Add device")
| extend ModifiedProperties = parse_json(TargetResources)[0].modifiedProperties
| mv-expand ModifiedProperties
| where ModifiedProperties.displayName == "DeviceOSType"
| extend OldValue = tostring(ModifiedProperties.oldValue)
| extend NewValue = tostring(ModifiedProperties.newValue)
| where OldValue != NewValue
| extend Actor = tostring(InitiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName)
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, OldValue, NewValue, OperationName, Result
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: MDI: AD Group Policy Password Policy
description: Queries Microsoft Defender for Identity events to enumerate AD Group Policy objects related to password policies.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: MDI AD Group Policy Password Policy
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
IdentityDirectoryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType == "Group Policy Object modified"
| extend GPOName = tostring(AdditionalFields.GPOName)
| extend AttributeChanged = tostring(AdditionalFields.AttributeChanged)
| extend OldValue = tostring(AdditionalFields.OldValue)
| extend NewValue = tostring(AdditionalFields.NewValue)
| where GPOName has_any ("Password", "Default Domain", "Account Policy")
    or AttributeChanged has_any ("minPwdLength", "maxPwdAge", "lockoutThreshold", "pwdHistoryLength")
| project Timestamp, GPOName, AttributeChanged, OldValue, NewValue, AccountName, DeviceName
| order by Timestamp desc

title: Sentinel Watchlists Overview
description: Lists all Microsoft Sentinel watchlists and their entry counts, useful for auditing watchlist coverage and identifying stale or empty watchlists.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Power Platform Customer Lockbox Activity
description: Monitors Power Platform admin activity for Customer Lockbox events, which track Microsoft engineer access requests to customer data.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: Power Platform Customer Lockbox Activity
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
PowerPlatformAdminActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
| where EventOriginalType has_any ("CustomerLockbox", "Lockbox")
| extend RequestId = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalInfo).RequestId)
| extend RequestState = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalInfo).RequestState)
| project TimeGenerated, ActorName, EventOriginalType, RequestId, RequestState, IPAddress
| union (
    CloudAppEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(90d)
    | where ActionType has "CustomerLockbox"
    | project TimeGenerated, AccountDisplayName, ActionType, IPAddress
)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: Copilot Agents: Sharing Activity
description: Tracks sharing events for Microsoft Copilot agents across CloudAppEvents and IdentityInfo to audit who is sharing agents and with whom.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Copilot Agent Approval Events
description: Tracks approval events for Copilot agents via OfficeActivity and CloudAppEvents, auditing who approved or rejected agent deployments.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: Copilot Agent Approval Events
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where Operation has_any ("AgentApproved", "AgentRejected", "CopilotAgentApproval")
| extend AgentName = tostring(parse_json(ExtendedProperties)[0].Value)
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, Operation, AgentName, ClientIP, UserAgent
| union (
    CloudAppEvents
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType has_any ("AgentApproved", "AgentRejected")
    | extend AgentName = tostring(RawEventData.AgentName)
    | project TimeGenerated, AccountDisplayName, ActionType, AgentName, IPAddress
)
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: Copilot Agents: Allowed Agent Types
description: Queries CloudAppEvents to enumerate what types of Copilot agents have been allowed or configured in the tenant.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Copilot Agents: User Access
description: Audits which users have been granted access to specific Copilot agents, combining CloudAppEvents with IdentityInfo for enrichment.
author: Alex Verboon

title: Unusual User Agent from Registered Device Bypassing Conditional Access
description: Detects sign-ins from registered devices using unusual user agents that differ from what Conditional Access expects, potentially indicating bypass attempts.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos

title: Defender RedSun: Named Pipe Detection Correlated to AV
description: Detects the RedSun named pipe technique correlated with antivirus detections, indicating possible defense evasion via named pipe injection.
author: Jay Kerai

title: List of MFA Methods Used with UPN Details
description: Enumerates all MFA methods in use across the tenant from SigninLogs, with user UPN details for each method.
author: Nathan Hutchinson
content:
// Title: List of MFA Methods Used with UPN Details
// Author: Nathan Hutchinson
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where isnotempty(AuthenticationDetails)
| mv-expand AuthStep = parse_json(AuthenticationDetails)
| extend MFAMethod = tostring(AuthStep.authenticationMethod)
| extend MFAResult = tostring(AuthStep.succeeded)
| where MFAMethod != "Previously satisfied"
| where MFAResult == "true"
| summarize UserCount = dcount(UserPrincipalName), Users = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 10) by MFAMethod
| order by UserCount desc

title: MDXDR Critical Assets
description: Identifies critical assets in Microsoft Defender XDR using ExposureGraphNodes and IdentityInfo, highlighting high-value targets.
author: Alex Verboon
content:
// Title: MDXDR Critical Assets
// Author: Alex Verboon
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
ExposureGraphNodes
| where NodeLabel == "device"
| extend CriticalityLevel = tostring(NodeProperties.rawData.criticalityLevel)
| where CriticalityLevel in ("Critical", "High")
| extend AssetName = tostring(NodeProperties.rawData.displayName)
| extend OS = tostring(NodeProperties.rawData.operatingSystemPlatform)
| extend ExposureScore = todouble(NodeProperties.rawData.exposureScore)
| join kind=leftouter (IdentityInfo | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated,*) by AccountObjectId | project AccountObjectId, AccountUpn, JobTitle, Department) on $left.NodeId == $right.AccountObjectId
| project AssetName, CriticalityLevel, OS, ExposureScore, AccountUpn, Department
| order by ExposureScore desc

title: Defender RedSun: TieringEngineService Created in AppData
description: Detects the RedSun TieringEngineService binary being created in AppData, a known indicator of the RedSun malware loader.
author: Jay Kerai

title: Suspicious Device Code Authentication
description: Detects suspicious device code authentication flows across SigninLogs, AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, and ADFSSignInLogs that may indicate device code phishing.
author: Jose Sebastian Canos

title: Users Authenticating with the MFA Companion App
description: Identifies users signing in using the Microsoft Authenticator Companion app, useful for auditing non-standard MFA methods.
author: Nathan Hutchinson

title: Overview of All MFA Methods in Use
description: Provides a tenant-wide overview of all MFA authentication methods in use, with counts per method.
author: Nathan Hutchinson
content:
// Title: Overview of All MFA Methods in Use
// Author: Nathan Hutchinson
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where ResultType == "0"
| where isnotempty(AuthenticationDetails)
| mv-expand AuthStep = parse_json(AuthenticationDetails)
| extend MFAMethod = tostring(AuthStep.authenticationMethod)
| extend MFASucceeded = tostring(AuthStep.succeeded)
| where MFAMethod !in ("Previously satisfied", "Password")
| summarize
    UniqueUsers = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
    TotalSignIns = count(),
    SuccessRate = countif(MFASucceeded == "true") * 100 / count()
    by MFAMethod
| order by UniqueUsers desc

title: Suspicious 0-Day Adobe Reader Process Activity
description: Detects suspicious network connections and file writes by Adobe Reader processes, indicative of a potential zero-day exploitation attempt.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Suspicious 0-Day Adobe Reader Process Activity
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// MITRE: T1203 - Exploitation for Client Execution
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
let AdobeProcesses = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("AcroRd32.exe", "Acrobat.exe", "AdobeARM.exe")
| project DeviceId, DeviceName, AdobePID = ProcessId, AdobeTime = Timestamp;
let SuspiciousNetwork = DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("AcroRd32.exe", "Acrobat.exe")
| where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
| where RemotePort !in (80, 443)
| project DeviceId, NetTime = Timestamp, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl;
let SuspiciousFiles = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("AcroRd32.exe", "Acrobat.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any ("Temp", "AppData", "Downloads")
| where FileName endswith_any (".exe", ".dll", ".ps1", ".bat")
| project DeviceId, FileTime = Timestamp, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256;
AdobeProcesses
| join kind=inner SuspiciousNetwork on DeviceId
| join kind=inner SuspiciousFiles on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (AdobeTime .. AdobeTime + 5m)
| where FileTime between (AdobeTime .. AdobeTime + 5m)
| project DeviceName, AdobeTime, RemoteIP, RemotePort, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256
| order by AdobeTime desc

title: Detect TLS Validation Bypass via PowerShell
description: Detects PowerShell commands that disable TLS certificate validation, a common technique used by malware to bypass SSL inspection.
author: Sergio Albea

title: Audit Claude AI Behavior on Endpoints
description: Detects process execution and file activity patterns associated with Claude AI (Anthropic) running on endpoints, for auditing AI tool usage.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Git Force Push Detection
description: Detects git force push commands on endpoints, which can be used to rewrite repository history and cover tracks.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Git Commit --amend --no-verify Detection
description: Detects git commit amendment with the --no-verify flag, which bypasses pre-commit hooks and may be used to sneak in malicious code.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Batch Git Abuse Detection
description: Detects execution of multiple git abuse techniques in batch (cmd.exe or scripts) within a short time window on the same device.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Defense Evasion: Git Time Change
description: Detects system time changes occurring shortly before or after git commit activity, which may indicate an attempt to falsify commit timestamps.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Defense Evasion: Git Config Masquerade
description: Detects git config changes to user.name or user.email that may be used to masquerade as another developer when making commits.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Correlation: Git and VS Code Task Abuse
description: Correlates git abuse commands launched from VS Code tasks, which may indicate malicious use of IDE automation for supply chain tampering.
author: Ali Hussein

title: IFEO: Unauthorized Debugger Registration
description: Detects Image File Execution Options (IFEO) registry modifications that register unexpected debuggers, a common persistence and privilege escalation technique.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: macOS: Suspicious Shell or Process Execution from Browser
description: Detects shell or direct process spawning from browser processes on macOS, which may indicate exploitation of a browser vulnerability.
author: Benjamin Zulliger

title: Device TVM Secure Configuration Assessment Summary
description: Summarizes the Threat and Vulnerability Management (TVM) secure configuration assessment results per device, highlighting non-compliant configurations.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
content:
// Title: Device TVM Secure Configuration Assessment Summary
// Author: Benjamin Zulliger
// Source: https://www.kqlsearch.com
// KQL Library - https://github.com/sivolko/kql-hunter
DeviceTvmSecureConfigurationAssessment
| where Timestamp > ago(1d)
| where IsCompliant == false
| join kind=leftouter (DeviceTvmSecureConfigurationAssessmentKB | project ConfigurationId, ConfigurationName, ConfigurationDescription, RiskDescription, Tags)
    on ConfigurationId
| summarize
    FailedConfigs = dcount(ConfigurationId),
    FailedConfigNames = make_set(ConfigurationName, 10),
    HighRiskCount = countif(Tags has "high")
    by DeviceId, DeviceName
| order by FailedConfigs desc

title: Node C2 Polinder Detection
description: Detects suspicious outbound network connections from Node.js processes that may indicate C2 communication via a polinder-style payload.
author: Ali Hussein

title: Microsoft Defender Cloud App Discovery to Log Analytics
description: Exports Microsoft Defender Cloud App Discovery data to a custom Log Analytics table for extended retention and custom alerting.
author: Benjamin Zulliger
